news.summary: A groundbreaking project is set to be initiated by researchers from the University of Connecticut and Eascra Biotech at Kennedy Space Center. This venture aims to enhance nanomaterial production utilizing the International Space Station (ISS) National Lab. The researchers plan to focus on improving the manufacturing process for potential treatments of osteoarthritis and cancer. The project, scheduled to launch on April 17, 2025, signifies a significant step in nanotechnology research. Stay tuned for updates on this innovative initiative.
news.headline: Researchers to use ISS National Lab for nanomaterial production to develop osteoarthritis & cancer treatments.

news.summary: The Wyoming Stable Token Commission, authorized by the state to issue WYST stablecoin, may adjust language to comply with SEC guidelines. In a meeting, suggestions were made to mitigate the risk of WYST being classified as a security. The SEC had stated "covered stablecoins" were mostly non-securities. The commission plans to discuss this further in a May memo. Governor Mark Gordon aimed for a Q1 2025 launch but may delay to July. Wyoming is monitoring federal regulations on stablecoins, with proposed bills like the GENIUS Act and STABLE Act. Wyoming, with 600,000 people, attracts crypto firms with favorable regulations. Custodia Bank by Caitlin Long is in Cheyenne. Senator Cynthia Lummis supports crypto-friendly policies.
news.headline: Wyoming considers SEC rules for state-issued stablecoin; commission looks to clarify language to avoid being labeled security. Gov. plans stablecoin launch in July, monitoring federal bills on stablecoin regulation. Wyoming attracts crypto firms in regulatory-friendly environment. Senator Lummis advocates for crypto policies.

news.headline: Pinnacle Mergers & Acquisitions sells Ocean Honda to Jerry Seiner Auto Group, 15th transaction with Victory Automotive Group.

news.summary: During a meeting with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, President Donald Trump renewed his criticism of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, suggesting he could replace him if rate cuts are not implemented soon. The meeting took place in the Oval Office and President Trump expressed dissatisfaction with Powell's handling of monetary policy, accusing him of maintaining high interest rates despite signals of disinflation in key sectors. He emphasized his discontent with Powell, stating "If I want him out, he'll be out of there real fast." Prime Minister Meloni praised Trump's global leadership role during the discussions, focusing on trade, China, Ukraine, and transatlantic relations. Trump's public criticism of Powell came just hours after Europe announced another round of policy easing. The President insisted that Powell is consistently "too late" in his decisions and accused him of playing politics. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Trump threatens to oust Powell from Fed if rate cuts don't happen soon. Trump criticizes Powell for holding rates high despite disinflation signals. Italy praises Trump's global role. More on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Toronto, April 17, 2025 - Inventus Mining Corp. (TSXV: IVS) has revealed plans to seek approval from the TSX Venture Exchange for an early warrant exercise incentive initiative. The program aims to prompt the early exercise of up to 15,000,000 existing common share purchase warrants (referred to as "Eligible Warrants") that were initially distributed during the private placement financing conducted on November 6th, 2024. Each Eligible Warrant allows the holder to buy one common share of Inventus at $0.09 per share and has a expiration date set for November 6th, 2026.
news.headline: Inventus Mining Corp. (TSXV: IVS) to apply for early warrant exercise program for up to 15M warrants at $0.09 each.

news.summary: Goldman Sachs analyst John Mackay discussed Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE: KMI) following the release of its first-quarter earnings. The energy infrastructure company reported revenue of $4.24 billion, falling short of the anticipated $4.08 billion. Adjusted quarterly earnings were 34 cents per share, missing analyst predictions of 35 cents per share. Kinder Morgan projects full-year 2025 earnings to be $1.27 per share, slightly lower than the $1.28 per share estimated by Benzinga Pro. The company expects net income attributable to increase by 8% from 2024. Mackay attributed the underperformance to weaker product performance. Access the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Goldman Sachs analyst positive on Kinder Morgan - Q1 revenue $4.24B, earnings 34 cents per share, 2025 EPS $1.27.

news.summary: The price of gold reached a record high of $3,357 per ounce on April 17, sparking speculation about Bitcoin's potential rise. Historically, Bitcoin has surged after gold spikes, with Bitcoin hitting $19,120 in 2017 following a 30% gold increase, and reaching $69,000 in 2021 after gold soared to $2,075 in 2020 during the pandemic. Analysts predict Bitcoin may hit new highs in late 2025, with a target price of $400,000. Galaxy Digital CEO Mike Novogratz sees both Bitcoin and gold as crucial indicators of economic uncertainty. Bitcoin's correlation with gold prices suggests a shift towards safe-haven assets amid rising global economic concerns. Novogratz warns of the US economy resembling an emerging market, with Bitcoin and gold reflecting unease over mounting deficits and a weakening dollar. This article doesn't provide investment advice, urging readers to conduct their own research before making financial decisions.
news.headline: Gold hitting new high at $3,357/oz spurs speculation on Bitcoin follow-up within 150 days. Bitcoin reached $69,000 in '21 post gold $2,075 peak in '20. Analyst predicts BTC may hit $400k by Q4 2025, reflecting dynamic gold-Bitcoin relationship during economic uncertainty. Market turbulence boosts Bitcoin and gold appeal, signaling financial stewardship amid macroeconomic uncertainty. Global economic shifts, tariffs, and US policies contribute to mounting concerns over deficits and national debt. Bitcoin and gold seen as safe havens in Minsky Moment for US economy.

news.summary: Woodside Energy Group Limited announced a landmark agreement with Uniper SE for LNG sale and purchase. The deal involves Louisiana LNG LLC providing 1.0 million tonnes per annum (Mtpa) of LNG on a free-on-board basis for a maximum of 13 years starting from the commercial operations date of the Louisiana LNG project. Furthermore, Woodside Energy Trading Singapore Pty. Ltd. will supply up to 1.0 Mtpa of LNG to Europe on a delivered ex-ship basis, commencing from the COD of the Louisiana LNG project and extending until 2039. These agreements will leverage Woodside's extensive global portfolio for energy trading.
news.headline: Woodside secures 2 Mtpa LNG offtake with Uniper for 13 years from Louisiana LNG project, shipping to Europe.

news.summary: Huaxia Fund in Hong Kong is launching staking services for its Ether exchange-traded fund (ETF) in partnership with OSL Digital Services, making it the second in the city to do so. The staking feature will be live on May 15, turning the ETF into an "active participant" in the Ethereum ecosystem. This move follows Hong Kong's Securities and Futures Commission's rule change on April 7 allowing crypto staking. Bosera HashKey was approved as the first staking provider in Hong Kong under the new rule. Staking involves locking up crypto tokens to support blockchain operations, and participants earn rewards in return. ETH stakers are currently earning about 2.14% yield on their holdings, according to Coinbase. Hong Kong aims to position itself as a Web3 hub, with a 250% growth in its blockchain sector since 2022. Other countries are also considering staking for Ether ETFs, with the US SEC reviewing applications by CBOE and NYSE for similar rules. Staking is seen as a way to attract more investors and enhance the appeal of Ether ETFs in the market.
news.headline: Huaxia Fund in HK plans Ether ETF staking with OSL, offering active participation, boosting Ethereum ecosystem on May 15. Staking providers grow post-SFC rule change for crypto, aiming to make HK Web3 hub. Earn 2.14% ETH yield, driving investor interest.

news.summary: In Akron, Ohio on April 17, 2025, the United States Special Operations Command (USSOCOM) has granted Cyberstar a substantial multi-year contract. This contract is for the provision of its cyber workforce management platform to aid USSOCOM's efforts in modernization. The platform aims to enhance compliance and readiness within USSOCOM. The specific financial value or duration of the contract was not disclosed.
news.headline: USSOCOM chooses Cyberstar for cyber workforce management platform for DCWF & 8140 modernization.

news.summary: The European Central Bank (ECB) reduced its benchmark deposit rate by 25 basis points, the seventh cut since June 2024. This decision was made due to the dynamics of inflation and monetary policy strength, with inflation predicted to stabilize at around 2%. Inflation had dropped from 10.6% in October 2022 to 2.6%, leading to rate cuts and a need to boost the economy due to decreased investment and spending caused by high interest rates. Key new rates are now 2.25%, 2.40%, and 2.65% for deposit facility, main refinancing operations, and marginal lending facility, respectively. Concerns over global uncertainties and trade tensions, including Trump administration tariffs, have influenced these moves. S&P Global revised down its 2025 global GDP growth projections by 0.3%. Global political instability, including tensions in Ukraine and the Middle East conflict, have also impacted ECB's decision-making. ECB governor Christine Lagarde highlighted the need to boost defence and infrastructure spending amid these challenges.
news.headline: ECB cuts rates by 25 basis points, marks 7th cut since June 2024 amid concerns on Eurozone growth and Trump tariffs. ECB lowers deposit facility rate to 2.25%, MRO rate to 2.40%, and marginal lending facility rate to 2.65%. S&P Global downgrades 2025 global GDP growth projections to 2.2% due to trade tensions. ECB seeks to protect eurozone from global uncertainty, with concerns about political instability influencing decision-making. ECB governor, Christine Lagarde, cites geopolitical tensions as major sources of uncertainty.

news.summary: Reproductive Partners Medical Group (RPMG), renowned in fertility care and IVF, is moving its Redondo Beach clinic to a modern facility in Torrance, California on April 14, 2025. news.headline: Reproductive Partners Medical Group relocating to new state-of-the-art facility in Torrance, CA on April 14th, 2025, after over 30 years in Redondo Beach.

This move towards unionization highlights a growing trend among LRS employees, showing a significant percentage of workers coming together to secure their rights and negotiate better working conditions. This development holds great promise for workers in the sanitation sector in the region.
news.headline: LRS mechanics in West Chicago join Teamsters Local 673, marking the third unit to unionize with the Teamsters.
